title: Which financial statement's structure is closest to that of the basic accounting equation?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which financial statement's structure is closest to that of the basic accounting equation?

Income Statement

Statement Of Stockholders’ Equity

Balance Sheet

Retained Earnings

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Your firm has a risk-free investment opportunity where it can invest $140,000 today and receive $150,000 in one year. For what level of interest rates is this project attractive?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
To determine the level of interest rates that would make this project attractive, we need to calculate the net present value (NPV) of the investment opportunity using different discount rates:

NPV = -$140,000 + $150,000 / (1 + r)

Where r is the annual interest rate. 

Simplifying the equation, we get:

NPV = -$140,000 + $150,000 / (1 + r) = $0 

Now we can solve for r:

$150,000 / (1 + r) = $140,000

1 + r = $150,000 / $140,000

1 + r = 1.0714

r = 0.0714 or 7.14%

Therefore, for interest rates below 7.14%, this investment opportunity is attractive, since the NPV would be positive. For interest rates above 7.14%, the NPV would be negative, indicating that the project would not generate a return that compensates for its initial cost.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
The changes that occurred during a recent year in the accounts Retained Earnings and Treasury Stock will be found in which financial statement?

Statement Of Stockholders’ Equity

Income Statement

Balance Sheet

Accounts Receivable

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
